skill.name: grafana-dashboards
skill.description: Create production Grafana dashboards for application, infrastructure, SLO, and business metric monitoring.
skill.summary: Design Grafana dashboards with panels, variables, alerts, and provisioning examples.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high and medium patterns, but review found documentation examples rather than executable code. The skill is a single Markdown file with Grafana dashboard guidance, JSON examples, Prometheus queries, Terraform snippets, and Ansible snippets. No malicious intent, prompt injection, network exfiltration, or runnable command execution was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: VERDICT: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines are Markdown code fences, inline file references, JSON examples, YAML examples, and related skill names. I found no shell execution, Ruby backticks, command runner, or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are visible Markdown documentation and fenced examples. There is no executable source file or command invocation context in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Prometheus Regex Flagged as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: VERDICT: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged line is a Prometheus query matching HTTP 5xx status codes. It does not read files, construct paths, or traverse directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains a dashboard query expression inside JSON. The apparent traversal pattern is part of a regular expression for status codes, not filesystem access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Keywords
low_findings.description: VERDICT: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain descriptive text, dashboard headings, file references, or provisioning fields. I found no cryptographic operation, hash use, system probing, or reconnaissance logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of each location found ordinary Grafana documentation content. No code performs hashing, encryption, host inspection, or data collection.

content.value_statement: Teams need dashboards that reveal service health without noisy or confusing panels. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design Grafana dashboards with metrics, variables, alerts, and provisioning patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains dashboard layouts for critical metrics, trends, and detailed panels.
Provides Grafana panel examples for stat, graph, table, and heatmap views.
Shows Prometheus query patterns for services, resources, latency, and errors.
Documents dashboard variables for namespace and service filtering.
Includes alert configuration guidance for error-rate thresholds.
Covers provisioning patterns using file providers, Terraform, and Ansible.
limitations: Does not include executable automation or generated dashboard files beyond Markdown examples.
Requires users to adapt metric names, labels, and data sources to their environment.
Does not validate dashboards against a live Grafana instance.
References resource and asset files that are not present in this scanned package.

use_cases.title: Create service health dashboards
use_cases.description: Design RED-method dashboards that show request rate, errors, and latency for production services.

use_cases.title: Standardize infrastructure views
use_cases.description: Plan reusable panels for CPU, memory, disk, network, pods, and node status across environments.

use_cases.title: Prepare dashboard-as-code rollout
use_cases.description: Map dashboard JSON, folders, provisioning paths, and deployment steps before implementing automation.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner dashboard outline
prompt_templates.scenario: Start a new Grafana dashboard from monitoring goals.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline a Grafana dashboard for my service. Include panels for rate, errors, latency, and basic resource usage.

prompt_templates.title: Prometheus panel plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Convert known metrics into Grafana panel ideas.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design Grafana panels for these Prometheus metrics: [paste metric names]. Include panel titles, units, and query intent.

prompt_templates.title: SLO monitoring dashboard
prompt_templates.scenario: Build a dashboard for reliability targets and alert review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan an SLO dashboard for availability and latency. Include burn-rate views, error panels, and alert thresholds.

prompt_templates.title: Provisioned dashboard rollout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a dashboard-as-code rollout plan for Grafana. Cover folders, variables, provisioning files, Terraform, and validation checks.

output_examples.input: Design an API monitoring dashboard for Prometheus metrics.
output: A dashboard structure with request rate, error percentage, and latency trend panels.
Recommended variables for service and namespace filtering.
Alert guidance for sustained high error rates.
A checklist of missing resource signals such as saturation and node status.
Suggestions for clearer panel grouping and consistent units.
Verification steps for multiple time ranges.
A deployment sequence for dashboard files, folders, and provider configuration.
Notes about adapting paths and permissions for the target Grafana host.
Post-deployment checks for refresh behavior and panel data.

best_practices: Start with user goals, data sources, and incident workflows before choosing panels.
Use consistent names, units, thresholds, and colors across related dashboards.
Test dashboards across time ranges and variable selections before production use.
anti_patterns: Do not copy example queries without matching metric names and labels.
Do not mix unrelated service, infrastructure, and business metrics in one crowded view.
Do not treat Markdown examples as validated production dashboard definitions.

faq.question: Does this skill create runnable Grafana dashboards automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides design guidance and examples that users must adapt and implement.

faq.question: Which data source does the skill focus on?
faq.answer: The examples focus mainly on Prometheus metrics, with general Grafana dashboard practices.

faq.question: Can it help with alerts?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for dashboard alert configuration and threshold planning.

faq.question: Can I use it for infrastructure monitoring?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers common infrastructure panels for CPU, memory, disk, network, pods, and nodes.

faq.question: Does it require network or file access?
faq.answer: No. The scanned package is Markdown guidance and does not execute network or filesystem operations.

faq.question: What should I verify before publishing a dashboard?
faq.answer: Verify metric names, label filters, units, thresholds, permissions, and behavior across time ranges.
